proposition2.group: Decision-Maker Contact Details & Project & Product Details
columns: Fencing Requirement Type (High-Security Fencing/Anti-Climb Systems/Smart Perimeter Security/Integrated Surveillance Fencing/Anti-Drone Security Systems)

records.customerName: Fraport AG Frankfurt Airport Services Worldwide
records.industryVertical: Airport Operator / Aviation Infrastructure / Critical Transport Infrastructure
records.existingSuppliers: Azkoyen Group / primion for access control and security management at Frankfurt Airport Terminal 3; Smiths Detection CT scanners used with APIDS aviation security technology
records.projectType: Upgrade / Retrofitting / Maintenance Contract / Future Construction Package
records.projectName: Frankfurt Airport Terminal 2 Refurbishment and Modernization
records.projectLocation: Frankfurt Airport, Frankfurt am Main, Hesse, Germany
records.projectStatus: Terminal 2 closed for refurbishment on June 9, 2026; detailed construction planning underway; main construction expected from 2030; reopening planned in mid-2030s
records.projectValue: Approx. €1.5 billion, equivalent to around US$1.6 billion
records.fencingRequirementType: High-Security Fencing; Anti-Climb Systems; Smart Perimeter Security; Integrated Surveillance Fencing; access-controlled site perimeter systems
records.productCategoryProcured: Anti-climb perimeter fencing, high-security gates, vehicle access gates, construction-zone perimeter fencing, PIDS-ready fencing, CCTV-integrated fencing, access-control barriers
records.currentSecurityTechnology: APIDS AI-based aviation security software, CT scanners from Smiths Detection, access control, staff identification, visitor management, airport ID and vehicle access control systems
records.supplierSwitchingProbability: Medium – Terminal 2 modernization creates scope for new vendors, but airport security suppliers need strong compliance, references, and integration capability
records.priceSensitivity: Medium to Low – safety, compliance, uptime, and integration are likely more important than lowest price
records.procurementDecisionStage: Vendor registration / tender monitoring / prequalification stage
records.currentFencingProvider: Regional aviation security EPC / access control integrators
records.productPortfolioUsed: Access gate control, staff ID systems, visitor management, credential management, CT-based security screening, AI-supported prohibited-item detection
records.estimatedContractValue: Fencing package not publicly disclosed; comparable Terminal 3 access/security tender won by Azkoyen was nearly €4 million, approx. US$4.3 million
records.keyCompetitiveAdvantage: Airport-grade compliance, proven aviation references, integration with access control/CCTV/PIDS, ability to meet EU procurement and airport safety requirements
records.renewalOpportunityTimeline: 2026 onward for Terminal 3 O&M/security maintenance; 2026–2030 for Terminal 2 modernization tender watch
records.tenderName: Frankfurt Airport Terminal 2 modernization – security, technical systems, access control, and perimeter-related construction packages
records.releaseDate: Specific fencing tender release not publicly disclosed; Fraport directs bidders to xvergabe.de for tender participation
records.qualificationCriteria: xvergabe registration, EU/SektVO procurement compliance, airport/critical infrastructure references, security clearance capability, supply-chain due diligence, technical integration capability
records.estimatedProcurementBudget: Overall Terminal 2 project budget: approx. €1.5 billion; fencing/security-perimeter package budget not publicly disclosed
records.bidParticipants: Aviation security EPC firms, perimeter specialists (Best Effort Basis)

records.projectName: Deutsche Bahn AG Perimeter Security Upgrade
records.fencingRequirementType: Wire and Mesh Fencing; Palisade Fencing; Barrier Systems
